name: Chicken Francaise
recipeIngredient: Lemons - peeled and sliced into thin rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat eggs in a bowl, about 1 egg per 2 pieces of chicken.
2. Add milk, garlic salt, and Lowry's seasoning (pepper) to the eggs.
3. Gradually add Wondra flour until the batter is thick but pourable. Do not substitute with regular flour.
4. Peel lemons and thinly slice into rounds.
5. Place regular flour in a shallow pan.
6. Heat frying pan with a combination of butter, margarine, and 1 tbsp vegetable oil.
7. Dust chicken pieces with regular flour, shaking off excess.
8. Dip floured chicken into the batter, allowing excess to drip off.
9. Saute chicken until browned. Place cooked chicken in a serving dish with lemon slices between each piece.
10. Increase heat on frying pan and add 2 cups of dry white wine.
11. Allow wine to bubble and cook off alcohol.
12. Pour some wine over the chicken and serve with additional wine in a gravy boat.
